calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2024 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-14-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206
Off Calendar Jun-26-2025 Continued To Oct-16-25 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 304

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Veronica Williams As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substituting Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff; For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; And To Add Previously Sued Doe Defendants To The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' Motion for Order granting leave to file First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. = (EPS/304).

ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing for Plaintiff;
Laura Przetak, Esq. of Spanos Przetak, Tel. 510.250.0200, appearing as Designated Defense Counsel;
D. of K&L Gates LLP, Tel. 310.552.5000, appearing for Defendant Redco Corporation fka Crane Co.;
Paul Smith, Esq. of Husch Blackwell LLP, Tel. 510.768.0650, appearing for Defendant Metalclad Insulation LLC;
Thomas Steig, Esq. of Prindle Goetz Barnes & Reinholtz LLP, Tel. 415.788.8354, appearing for Intervenors Hartford Accident and Indemnity Company, Hartford Casualty Insurance Company, Argonaut Insurance Company, and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, insurers of Defendant SYD Carpenter, Marine Contractor Inc.;
Robert Bugatto, Esq. of Hugo Parker LLP, Tel. 415.808.0300, appearing for Defendat Foster Wheeler LLC fka Foster Wheeler Corporation.

A Case Management Conference set per preference order is held.

Ms. Chu informs the Court that the plaintiff has passed away and that the matter was kept on calendar to discuss next steps. Discussions are held.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court vacates the following dates: the Trial on April 14, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 206 and the Mandatory Settlement Conference on April 10, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 503. The Court adds the matter to June 26, 2025, 1:30 p.m. Dept. 304 Trial Setting Conference calendar. No objection counsel. The Court will issue the order.

Ms. Chu states that they hope to have a successor-in-interest by the Trial Setting Conference date. Ms. Przetak notes that plaintiff can amend the complaint one time without leave of court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (304)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any party or counsel.

The matter is not contested. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:
(304/EPS)

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). No opposition filed.

1. The trial date is April 14, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is April 23, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 8, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is March 28, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is April 11, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 23, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
8. No later than January 7, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
(Part 1 of 2)
(Mini Minutes continued on the following entry)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (Part 2 of 2)
(Mini Minutes Continued from previous entry)

9. A further case management conference is set for February 25, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than March 7, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due March 21, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on March 21, 2025.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, April 10, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: K. Septien. Matter not Reported. Bailiff not present. (304/EPS)
